get.description: Basic health check endpoint.
Readiness check including database connectivity.
Liveness check for container orchestration.

post.description: Sign in a user and return authentication tokens.
Sign up a new user.
Refresh an access token using a refresh token.
Verify if a token is valid and return user information.

get.description: Get current user information from token (placeholder for testing).

post.description: Create a new organization.

get.description: List organizations for the current user.
Get organization details.

delete.description:
Delete an organization and all its data.

Only organization owners can delete. This permanently removes:
- All nodes and their commit histories
- All branches, pull requests, and objects
- All projection data (tags, connections, visual properties)
- All organization memberships, invite links, and allowed emails

post.description: Add a member to organization.

get.description: List organization members.

post.description:
Resolve an invite token to org name and role.

No authentication required — used by the frontend to preview
what organization/role an invite link grants.

Accept an invite link to join an organization.

The user's email (from JWT claims) is checked against the allowlist
if the organization is in restricted mode.

get.description: Get invite links for an organization. Owner only.

post.description:
Regenerate an invite link token. Owner only.

The old token is invalidated immediately.

get.description: Get allowed emails for an organization. Owner only.

post.description: Add emails to the organization's allowlist. Owner only.

delete.description: Remove an email from the allowlist. Owner only.

get.description:
Get hierarchical directory structure across all organizations the user is a member of.

This endpoint is used by the frontend to show a unified directory tree view
across all organizations the user has access to.

parameters.name: root

schema.description: Root path to start tree from
schema.title: Root

parameters.description: Root path to start tree from

get.description:
Get node contents within a directory path across all organizations the user is a member of.

This endpoint is used by the frontend to show actual nodes within a specific directory
across all organizations the user has access to.
List all directories in organization.

Get node contents within a directory path for a specific organization.

This endpoint matches the frontend's expected URL pattern /orgs/{org_id}/directories/contents
and provides directory contents for a single organization.
Get hierarchical directory tree structure.

Get the containment hierarchy across all organizations the user belongs to.

Returns all nodes and their positions in the parent/child ownership
structure, scoped to orgs the user is a member of.

Get the containment hierarchy for an organization.

Returns all nodes and their positions in the parent/child ownership
structure, without user-assigned directories. Used by the CLI tree command.

List nodes across all organizations the user is a member of.

This endpoint is used by the frontend to show a unified view of all
nodes the user has access to across their organization memberships.

post.description: Create a new node in an organization.

get.description: List nodes for an organization.

delete.description:
Soft delete a node.

Marks the node as deleted in the projection layer so it no longer appears
in search results, node lists, or directory trees. All git history (commits,
objects, branches) is preserved. The node remains visible in parent canvases
that contain it.

put.description:
Git-like full-state push operation.

This endpoint implements the declarative API where clients send the complete
desired state, and the server handles diffing and object reuse automatically.

Similar to `git push`, the client describes the end state they want, and the
server figures out what changed and creates an optimal commit.

Features:
- Automatic object reuse for unchanged content
- Complete state snapshots in each commit
- Branch support for collaborative workflows
- Validation of state schema

get.description: Get the HEAD commit hash for a branch (lightweight staleness check).
Get complete state for a node at a specific branch for canvas rendering.

Get canvas state for a node at a specific branch with flat structure.

Returns a flat dictionary where keys are node IDs and values are complete
state objects. The parent node's child_nodes field contains only child node
IDs (not nested objects). Each child node gets its own entry in the dictionary.

Query params:
    resolve_children: "latest" (default) shows children at their current main HEAD.
                      "snapshot" shows children as they were when last pushed.

post.description:
Search nodes with flexible filters.

Accepts a JSON body with optional filter fields: tags, directories,
title_contains, created_by, min/max_children, min/max_parents, etc.
All filters are AND-ed together. For tags, tag_match_mode controls
whether all tags must match ("all") or any tag ("any").

get.description:
Get commit history for a node at a specific branch.

This endpoint matches the frontend's expected URL pattern:
/orgs/{org_id}/nodes/{node_id}/tree/{branch}/commits
Get commit history for a node.

Get detailed information about a specific commit including changes.

Similar to 'git show <commit>' - returns commit metadata plus the 
changes introduced by this commit compared to its parent.
Get diff between two commits for a node.

Get complete state for a node at a specific commit for canvas rendering.

This endpoint reconstructs the exact state as it was at the specified commit,
similar to 'git checkout <commit>'. It's useful for viewing historical states
or comparing how the node looked at different points in time.

Get canvas state for a node at a specific historical commit with flat structure.

Returns a flat dictionary where keys are node IDs and values are complete
state objects. The parent node's child_nodes field contains only child node
IDs (not nested objects). Each child node gets its own entry in the dictionary.

This endpoint is used for viewing historical canvas states - the canvas as it
existed at a specific point in time.

Query params:
    resolve_children: "latest" (default) shows children at their current main HEAD.
                      "snapshot" shows children as they were when this commit was pushed.

post.description: Create a new branch from an existing branch.

get.description: List all branches for a node.
Compare two branches for pull request creation.

delete.description: Delete a branch from a node.

post.description: Create new pull request (fast-forward only).

get.description: List pull requests for node.
Get specific pull request with merge status.

post.description: Merge pull request (fast-forward only).
Close pull request without merging.

get.description: Get graph across all organizations the user is a member of.
Get the complete org graph for the visualization map.

post.description: Submit a contact message. No authentication required.

Start an auto-create pipeline from GitHub IaC files.

Creates a job record, runs the pipeline, and returns the job ID.
Poll GET /orgs/{org_id}/auto-create/jobs/{job_id} for status.

get.description: List all auto-create jobs for an organization.
Get auto-create job status.
Get all nodes linked to an auto-create job.
Generate a signed state token for the GitHub App install redirect.

post.description: Save a GitHub App installation for an organization.

get.description: List all GitHub App installations created by the current user.
List all integrations for an organization, optionally filtered by provider type.

delete.description: Delete an integration. For GitHub, also handles uninstall from GitHub if last org.

get.description: Return Flow's Account ID, a fresh External ID, and the trust policy template.

post.description: Create an AWS integration and validate the connection via STS AssumeRole.
Re-validate an AWS integration by attempting STS AssumeRole.
Check if an AWS resource exists using the org's assumed-role credentials.
Describe an AWS resource — check existence and fetch tags.
Fetch CloudWatch metrics for an AWS resource.
Publish a signup notification. Email comes from JWT, not request body.
